nurse: This radical is made up of blackjack and clothes with the six kanji on top. If you see six people playing blackjack and wearing the same clothes you should know that they're all nurses. Nurses play blackjack together to cool down between shifts and they usually don't have time to change out of their uniforms. That's why this radical is nurse.
employ: This radical is the same as the kanji. It means employ.
every: This radical is the same as the kanji. It means every.
preserve: This radical is the same as the kanji. It means preserve.
signpost: This is a post with signs on it. And on top is a winter marker, showing that if you go north it's winter up there. The signs say things like "Narnia ↑" and "Meridell ←" and "Bananaland →" so you know it's a signpost.
melon: This is a melon. Imagine a melon cut in half, and you're looking at its insides from the side. See those precious private melon parts? You're gonna keep those for yourself, because melons are delicious and not for sharing.
cactus: See the cactus coming out of the ground? It's happy to see you!
shop: We already have a roof radical, so we're going to use the secondary kanji meaning for this radical. It's shop, remember?
satellite: Look at this radical. Can you see it floating in space? Can you see the antennae and reflectors? That's because it's a satellite.
imperial: Stand up on the moon so you can look down on your countries. The thing with all the lines and borders are the borders of all the countries you rule, for you are an imperial.
bone: This radical is the same as the kanji. It means bone.
plow: You're sliding some dirt around with a tree you cut down. This contraption you've made is called a plow. Use it to push dirt around!
badger: You know the animal radical. This is just like that with a little extra. It's an animal already... which means all you have to do is remember what kind of animal. In this case, it's a badger.
wind: This radical is the same as the kanji. It means wind.
can: It's kind of hard to see, but if you look at this the right way it looks like a food can. See the lid being pulled off in the top-left corner? And that horizontal line in the middle? That’s the can's label.
dragon: A turtle that stands up isn't a turtle anymore. It's evolved into a monster. What kind of monster? A dragon! Rawwwr.
business: This radical is the same as the kanji. It means business.
protect: This radical is the same as the kanji. It means protect.
wave: This radical is the same as the kanji. It means wave.
favor: This radical is the same as the kanji. It means favor.
house: Under this roof is a pig with her little ones. Your pig, because it's your house! You keep your piggies inside where it's nice and warm. They're all members of your family, so they deserve to live in your house too.
again: What's under the lid on your cook pot? Legs and fins. Again. All you know how to cook is legs (of chicken) and fins, so you cook them again and again and again. You'll be having them for dinner tonight, and again tomorrow, and again the next day. Legs and fins, again and again and again, for the rest of your days.
penguin: There are fins wrapped in a towel in winter. Who is drying these fish fins to eat them? A penguin.
form: This radical is the same as the kanji. It means form.
mysterious: What's under this lid that smells like poop? I don't know! How mysterious.
times: This radical is the same as the kanji. It means times.
height: You see a narwhal struggling with a measuring tape, trying to measure his height! His fins can't reach the end of the tape, but of course he wants to include his glorious tusk. Measuring height is not an easy tusk… er, task for a narwhal, but he'll figure it out. Probably.
crab-trap: This radical looks like a small animal (匹) has a crab (其) sticking out of it. Often when a crab is eating a small animal, that small animal was put inside a crab trap to catch the crab, luring it in with the small animal. That's why this radical is crab trap.
fat: This looks like the guy from the big radical, but he's got something extra on his belly. That's his exposed belly button, because he's a sumo wrestler now: big and fat. In sumo, more fat means more power, so imagine he's been training to get as strong and fat as possible.
psychopath: Inside this guy's forehead you have a spoon and a bunch of hair, not to mention whatever that is in the top left. That's the mind of a psychopath right there.
name: This radical is the same as the kanji. It means name.
splinter: Although a little bit modified, there is a turtle shell that's on a mustache with this radical. The only thing with a mustache that hangs around turtles is Splinter, the giant rat from Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles. So this radical is Splinter (the giant rat).
stomach: This radical is the same as the kanji. It means stomach.
frostbite: This radical is made of dirt, legs, and winter. If you have to bury your legs in the dirt in winter, it's probably because you got frostbite. You had to leave your dead legs behind. :(
tall: This radical is the same as the kanji. It means tall.
doubt: This radical is the same as the kanji. It means doubt.
convex: This looks like a box with something sticking out of it. When something is sticking out of something else it's convex.
concave: It looks like there is a dent in this box. When there is a hole or a dent in something it's called concave.
below: This radical is the same as the kanji. It means below.
courage: When a mama watches over a man, it gives him the courage he needs to take on any foe.
